title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Itchy Skin
intro: Herbal teas for itchy skin are a natural remedy that involves the use of plant-based teas to alleviate and soothe itchy skin conditions, such as eczema, dermatitis, and hives. These teas can treat itchy skin because they contain anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help to reduce itching and inflammation. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat itchy skin include peppermint tea, which helps to cool the skin and reduce itching; chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin; calendula tea, which has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that promote wound healing and reduce itching; and ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce itching and inflammation. Additionally, other herbal teas such as lavender tea, dandelion tea, and licorice root tea can also be used to treat itchy skin due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat itchy skin ailments such as eczema and dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce itching and alleviate skin irritation. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and polyphenols, in Urtica dioica tea exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. Regular consumption of Urtica dioica tea may help to soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers, or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves and flowers.
Combine the leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the solids.
Let the tea cool down before using it as a compress or applying it to itchy skin with a cloth.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating itchy skin ailments, but potential side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, take precautions by starting with small doses, avoiding consumption if you have sensitive skin or allergies, and keeping the teas away from children and pets, as well as not consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Urtica dioica leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day.
Apply a cold compress or wet cloth to itchy skin areas.
Use tea baths by adding 1 cup of the cooled tea to your bath water.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat itchy skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The flavonoids, carotenoids, and triterpenoids present in Calendula officinalis teas are responsible for its therapeutic effects, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. These bioactive constituents work together to alleviate symptoms of itchy skin, such as redness and itching, and help to restore the skin's natural barrier function. Regular consumption of Calendula officinalis teas has been shown to provide relief from itchy skin conditions, making it a popular natural remedy for skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Pour the boiling water over 1 tablespoon of dried Calendula flowers in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1 cup of the Calendula tea, 2-3 times a day, for itchy skin relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Calendula officinalis teas for itchy skin have a dermatologic importance of 57.78 on a scale of 100, indicating its potential effectiveness in treating itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments, but it may cause allergic reactions, such as skin redness, itching, or rashes, in some individuals. When using Calendula officinalis teas to treat itchy skin, it's essential to take precautions to avoid overusing the herbal preparation, as excessive consumption can lead to stomach upset, diarrhea, or interact with other medications.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Calendula officinalis tea for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean cup and let it cool.
Soak a cotton pad in the cooled tea and apply it to affected skin.
Leave the cotton pad on for 15-20 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
Repeat the process for 1-2 weeks to see desired results.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas, derived from the Aloe vera plant, have been used for centuries to treat various skin ailments, including itchy skin. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of this herbal preparation help to calm and reduce the itching sensation, providing relief to those affected. The bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamin E, work synergistically to reduce inflammation, promote skin hydration, and enhance wound healing, ultimately alleviating the itchy skin condition. Regular consumption of Aloe barbadensis teas has been found to provide numerous benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting skin elasticity, and enhancing overall skin health, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy skin.
preparation_procedure: Wash your hands and clean the area where you'll be handling aloe vera.
Take 1 cup of aloe vera gel from a fresh aloe vera leaf. If using a store-bought aloe vera gel, use 2-3 tablespoons.
Add 1 cup of water to the aloe vera gel in a pot.
Heat the mixture on low heat for 10-15 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the liquid and drink 1/4 cup of the aloe vera tea 2-3 times a day to soothe itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Forschende Komplementarmedizin", Aloe barbadensis teas for itchy skin have shown potential as a treatment option, although further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and record side effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments, but it may cause some side effects such as mild skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. When using Aloe barbadensis teas for this purpose, it is recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming it before or after other medications, as this may interact with them and exacerbate the side effects.
preparation_usage: Wash itchy skin with soap and water to clean it.
Mix 1-2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis gel with 1 cup of water.
Soak a cloth in the mixture and apply it to itchy skin.
Leave the cloth on for 15-30 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
Drink 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis tea, 1-2 times a day.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat the itchy skin ailment known as eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides and phenolic acids, help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and itchiness. These herbal preparations work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the development of eczema, and by modulating the immune system to reduce the severity of symptoms. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin barrier function, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of outbreaks.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to combine.
Strain the tea into another cup, using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe itchy skin, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be beneficial in treating itchy skin ailments, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, it's essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid taking them for extended periods of time, as prolonged use may lead to dependency and interact with other medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea, 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of Echinacea purpurea tincture, 3 times a day.
Use a cold compress on itchy skin for 15-20 minutes.
Bathe with an oatmeal bath once a day for itchy skin relief.
Combine Echinacea with oatmeal for enhanced itchy skin relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat the itchy skin ailment of eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, thereby alleviating the symptoms of eczema. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting wound healing, Hypericum perforatum teas provide relief from the itchy, red, and swollen skin associated with eczema. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat eczema include reduced inflammation, improved skin hydration, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of eczema flare-ups.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers, 1 cup of boiling water, and a heat-resistant cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers to the heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers in the cup.
Cover the cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day to help with itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC veterinary research", Hypericum perforatum teas for itchy skin may be beneficial due to its antibacterial and antifungal effects, which can help alleviate skin infections that may be causing the itchiness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments such as eczema and dermatitis, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. Precautions to take when using Hypericum perforatum teas include avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as being cautious when taking other medications that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Hypericum perforatum tea for itchy skin.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Hypericum perforatum in a cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 3 times a week, for 2 weeks.
Monitor skin condition and adjust dosage or consult a doctor if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been used to treat itchy skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and relieve itching, making it an effective remedy for skin irritations. The tea's antiseptic and antibacterial properties also aid in preventing infections and promoting wound healing, further alleviating symptoms of itchy skin. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat itchy skin include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a soothing effect that provides relief from discomfort and itching.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while warm to help soothe itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments, but they may cause side effects such as high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and changes in blood sugar levels, particularly in individuals with pre-existing conditions. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is essential to be cautious of excessive consumption, as it can lead to an over-supply of licorice compounds in the body, and to gradually discontinue use if symptoms persist or worsen.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried root powder with tea, 2-3 times a day.
Soak itchy skin areas in warm water with 1 teaspoon of root powder.
Apply a paste made from 1 teaspoon of root powder and water to itchy skin.
Continue using for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvement in itchy skin.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion teas, have been used to treat the itchy skin ailment known as eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the itching and inflammation associated with eczema. By reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, Taraxacum officinale teas provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with eczema, allowing the skin to heal and recover. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat eczema include reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and a decrease in the need for conventional medications, making it a popular natural remedy for this common skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers. You can grow them in your garden or buy them from a store.
Dry the Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers completely. You can air dry them or use a food dehydrator.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers for every cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried Taraxacum officinale in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Taraxacum officinale tea 2-3 times a day to relieve itchy skin symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat itchy skin ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, using Taraxacum officinale teas may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interact with certain medications, and precautions should be taken to avoid overusing it, especially in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and those with liver or kidney problems, as excessive consumption may lead to liver damage or other complications.
preparation_usage: Wash itchy skin area with soap and water
Steep 2 teaspoons of Taraxacum officinale in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes
Strain the liquid and let it cool down to body temperature
Soak a cloth in the cooled liquid and apply it to itchy skin for 15 minutes, 3 times a day
Drink 1/2 cup of the cooled liquid 2 times a day as an oral supplement

preparations.herb_desc: Althaea officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat itchy skin ailments due to their soothing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat itchy skin by providing relief from redness and irritation, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Althaea officinalis, including mucilages and flavonoids, play a crucial role in treating itchy skin by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface and exerting anti-inflammatory effects. The benefits of using Althaea officinalis teas to treat itchy skin include reduced discomfort, accelerated healing, and prevention of further irritation, making it a popular natural remedy for skin conditions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2-3 tablespoons of dried Althaea officinalis root.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to sweeten it.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe itchy skin. You can also use it as a compress by soaking a cloth in the tea and applying it to the affected area.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. Discard any remaining tea after 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Althaea officinalis teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea or stomach cramps, and allergic reactions like skin rashes or itching. To use Althaea officinalis teas safely, be cautious when consuming it in large quantities, as it may interact with other medications or exacerbate underlying conditions like kidney problems or bleeding disorders.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Althaea officinalis tea, 2-3 times a day.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to itchy skin.
Leave the cloth on itchy skin for 15-30 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
Repeat the process for 3-5 days to see noticeable results.
If needed, consult a doctor for further guidance or treatment.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Symphytum officinale teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments such as eczema, but it may cause gastrointestinal side effects like nausea and stomach discomfort due to its expectorant properties. Use 2-3 tablespoons of dried herb per cup of boiling water.

Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen.

Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can help alleviate itchy skin symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties, but possible side effects may include digestive issues such as nausea and stomach upset, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes or itching. To use Cinchona officinalis teas safely, it is essential to start with small doses, avoid mixing with other medications or herbs, and be cautious of interactions with other health conditions, particularly those affecting the liver or kidneys.

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas have been traditionally used to treat itchy skin ailments, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including ferulic acid and caffeic acid, help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, thereby alleviating symptoms of itchy skin. Equisetum arvense teas work by soothing and calming the skin, reducing redness and itching, and promoting the healing process. The benefits of using Equisetum arvense teas to treat itchy skin ailments include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a natural alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense leaves and stems.
Dry the Equisetum arvense leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 1-2 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried Equisetum arvense leaves and stems into a fine powder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the Equisetum arvense powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include being aware of its potential to exacerbate conditions like kidney or liver disease, and taking it in moderation, as excessive consumption may lead to adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas have been traditionally used to treat itchy skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this condition by reducing skin irritation and inflammation, promoting relaxation and calming the skin. Avena sativa teas contain bioactive constituents such as avenanthramides, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe itchy skin and reduce redness. The benefits of using Avena sativa teas to treat itchy skin include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa seeds. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Soak the seeds in 1 cup of water for 8-12 hours. Let them sit at room temperature.
Strain the water and rinse the seeds. Use a fine-mesh sieve to catch the seeds.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the soaked seeds in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day to help with itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Avena sativa teas to treat itchy skin, take precautions to monitor your body's reaction, start with low doses, and avoid using it if you have any underlying allergies or are pregnant.

preparations.herb_desc: Saponaria officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat itchy skin ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties, which help to calm and soothe irritated skin. The bioactive constituents of Saponaria officinalis, including saponins and flavonoids, have been found to reduce inflammation and promote healing, thereby alleviating itching and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Saponaria officinalis teas provide relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The benefits of using Saponaria officinalis teas to treat itchy skin ailments include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a soothing relief from itching and discomfort.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Saponaria officinalis leaves. You can also use 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add 1 tablespoon of Saponaria officinalis leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe itchy skin.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, Saponaria officinalis teas for itchy skin may be beneficial due to its significant anti-inflammatory activity, which was found to inhibit the production of IL-6 and TNF-α in human monocytic cell line (THP-1) cells, potentially helping to alleviate itchy skin conditions.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Saponaria officinalis teas can be used to treat itchy skin ailments, but using them may lead to side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased heart rate. Precautions to take when using Saponaria officinalis teas include avoiding them if you have sensitive skin, taking them in moderation, and being aware of any interactions with other medications, especially those affecting the heart and blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been used for centuries to treat itchy skin ailments such as eczema and dermatitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing skin irritation and promoting the healing of damaged skin tissue. The bioactive constituents of Centella asiatica teas, including triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, contribute to their therapeutic effects, including anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The benefits of using Centella asiatica teas to treat itchy skin ailments include reduced redness and itching, improved skin hydration, and enhanced skin elasticity, making it a popular natural remedy for skin conditions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Centella asiatica leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Centella asiatica leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink it warm or cool.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, Centella asiatica teas do not appear to be directly linked to the treatment of itchy skin in the provided study, which focuses on its wound healing properties and potential applications in the treatment of cutaneous wounds.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be effective in treating itchy skin ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Centella asiatica teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it if you have a known allergy to plants in the Apocynaceae family.

intro_study: According to "The Australasian journal of dermatology", teas for itchy skin may not be effective in achieving a mycological cure, but tea tree oil cream can reduce symptomatology as effectively as tolnaftate 1%.
